# API

# Authentication

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/api/authentication/

> Authenticate to api.qefro.com with user JWTs, widget tokens, or admin credentials.

**API authentication** uses HTTPS to `https://api.qefro.com`.

| Client | Credential |
| --- | --- |
| Admin Console / portal / most REST | `Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt>` |
| Website Widget | Widget token on WS query/`Authorization` |
| End-user for tools | `X-End-User-Token` or `X-End-User-Session` |
| Super Admin | Token from `POST /api/v1/admin/auth/login` |
| GraphQL | User JWT on `POST /graphql` |

## Introduction

Signup/login/OTP also available via GraphQL and REST auth handlers with Redis-backed abuse rate limits.

## Why it exists

Different surfaces need different trust levels without sharing Owner JWTs to browsers.

## Concepts

- User JWT / session jti (revocable)
- Widget token (rotatable publishable key)
- Forwarded end-user identity

## Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Client
  participant API
  Client->>API: Authorization Bearer
  API->>API: Resolve tenant + role
  API-->>Client: JSON / stream
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/billing/plans
```

```typescript
await fetch('https://api.qefro.com/graphql', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${userJwt}`,
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({ query: '{ __typename }' }),
});
```

## Best practices

- Store JWTs in memory/httpOnly patterns appropriate to your client
- Rotate widget tokens after incidents

## Security notes

Never commit JWTs or widget tokens to git. Prefer environment injection in CI.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# REST APIs

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/api/rest-apis/

> Versioned REST surface on api.qefro.com — uploads, billing, tools, widget, org RBAC, WhatsApp.

**REST APIs** complement GraphQL. Important groups:

### Health
- `GET /health`, `GET /ready`

### Widget
- `POST /api/v1/widget/chat`, settings, leads, feedback, handoff, identity/clear, STT/TTS
- `GET /widget.js`, `GET /ws/chat`

### Documents
- `POST /api/v1/documents`, `/documents/text`, `POST /documents/:id/reindex`

### Chat (authenticated users)
- `POST /api/v1/chat`, `/chat/stream`
- Conversations CRUD under `/api/v1/conversations/*`

### Billing (Razorpay)
- `GET /api/v1/billing/plans`
- `POST /api/v1/billing/checkout`, `/confirm`, `/cancel`
- `GET /api/v1/billing/subscription`, `/payments`
- `POST /api/v1/billing/webhook`

### Business Tools
- `/api/v1/workspaces/:id/integrations|tools` …
- `/api/v1/tools/:id/test|logs`

### Organization RBAC
- `/api/v1/org/*`, `/api/v1/team/*`

### WhatsApp
- `GET|POST /api/v1/whatsapp/webhook`

### Public
- `/api/v1/public/tenant-branding`, `tenant-slug-available`
- `/chat/:tenant_slug`

## Introduction

OpenAPI export can be added under `static/openapi/` as the contract freezes; this page lists the live route groups from the Rust API.

## Why it exists

Multipart uploads, webhooks, and streaming chat are awkward in pure GraphQL — REST covers them.

## Concepts

- `/api/v1` version prefix
- Tenant scoping via auth
- Quota middleware on messages/documents

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Clients --> REST
  Clients --> GQL[GraphQL /graphql]
  REST --> Services[Domain services]
  GQL --> Services
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

## Best practices

- Prefer idempotent clients on billing confirm
- Always send `workspace_id` where required

## Security notes

Webhook routes verify signatures / Meta challenges before mutating state.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# SDKs

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/api/sdks/

> Official widget package and HTTP examples for server integrations.

**SDKs** today center on the embeddable widget:

- npm: `@qefro-ai/widget`
- CDN: `https://cdn.qefro.com/widget.js`

Server integrations use ordinary HTTPS clients against `api.qefro.com` (REST + GraphQL).

## Introduction

The widget exposes `open`, `setContext`, `identify`, `setAuthToken`, and `clearIdentity`.

## Why it exists

Most customers embed chat in a website; npm/CDN coverage matters more than a dozen language SDKs.

## Concepts

- Widget SDK
- REST/GraphQL via fetch/curl

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  NPM["@qefro-ai/widget"] --> API
  CDN[widget.js] --> API
  Server[Your backend] --> API
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```bash
npm install @qefro-ai/widget
```

```javascript
const widget = new Widget({
  token: process.env.QEFRO_WIDGET_TOKEN,
  endpoint: 'https://api.qefro.com',
});
```

## Best practices

- Pin widget CDN versions in enterprise change control if you mirror the script
- Keep server secrets off the client

## Security notes

Do not ship Owner JWTs inside frontend bundles.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Examples

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/api/examples/

> cURL, TypeScript, and Python examples against production hosts.

**Examples** for common calls.

## Health

```bash
curl -sS https://api.qefro.com/health
```

## List plans

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/billing/plans
```

## Upload a document

```bash
curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  -F "file=@faq.pdf" -F "workspace_id=$WORKSPACE_ID" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/documents
```

## TypeScript widget identify

```typescript
widget.identify({
  id: user.id,
  email: user.email,
  auth: { mode: 'jwt', token: userJwt },
});
```

## Python plans

```python
import os, urllib.request
req = urllib.request.Request(
    "https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/billing/plans",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {os.environ['QEFRO_TOKEN']}"},
)
print(urllib.request.urlopen(req).read().decode())
```

## Related topics

---

# Rate Limits

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/api/rate-limits/

> Abuse controls and runtime rate limits in the Qefro API.

**Rate limits** protect auth and runtime surfaces:

| Area | Behavior (implementation) |
| --- | --- |
| Login / OTP / forgot / reset | Per-email Redis limits (e.g. login ~20/15m) |
| Widget HTTP | `rate_limit_middleware` on widget routes |
| WebSocket | ~30 messages / 60s style checks |
| Tool test | Limited per tenant (e.g. 60 window) |
| Plan quotas | Messages/documents/users/business tools |

Exceeding limits returns a rate-limited API error.

## Introduction

Quotas (`messages_limit`, `documents_limit`, …) are plan-level and enforced in middleware (`check_messages_limit_*`, `check_documents_limit`).

## Why it exists

Public widget endpoints and auth forms are internet-facing.

## Concepts

- Abuse rate limit
- Plan quota
- Tool test throttle

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Req --> Middleware
  Middleware --> Redis[(Redis counters)]
  Middleware --> Handler
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```bash
# Expect 429-style errors when limited — reduce chat spam in tests
```

## Best practices

- Cache plan list responses
- Don’t hammer `/tools/:id/test` in CI loops

## Security notes

Auth rate limits intentionally avoid account enumeration (constant responses on some flows).

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Error Codes

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/api/error-codes/

> API error shape and common failure modes.

**Error responses** typically include a machine-readable `code` and human `message` (see API `ApiError` JSON). Common situations:

| Situation | What to check |
| --- | --- |
| 401 | Missing/expired user JWT or widget token |
| 403 | RBAC — Member lacking workspace/team grant |
| 404 | Wrong id / tenant scoping |
| 429 | Rate limit or plan quota |
| 400 | Validation / bad signature (billing, WhatsApp) |

## Introduction

GraphQL errors follow GraphQL conventions; REST uses HTTP status + JSON body.

## Why it exists

Clients need stable codes for retries vs user-visible failures.

## Concepts

- `code` string
- HTTP status
- Validation errors

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Handler --> ApiError --> JSON
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```json
{
  "code": "FORBIDDEN",
  "message": "Insufficient permissions"
}
```

## Best practices

- Log `request` correlation ids when present
- Map 429 to exponential backoff

## Security notes

Do not echo raw upstream tool errors containing secrets to end users.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Webhooks

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/api/webhooks/

> Razorpay billing webhooks and WhatsApp Meta webhooks.

**Webhooks** Qefro consumes:

### Billing (Razorpay)
- `POST /api/v1/billing/webhook`
- Verifies Razorpay signature
- Handles events including `payment.captured`, `payment.failed`, subscription lifecycle events
- Failed payments stored with `error_code` / `error_reason` / `error_description`
- Activation is idempotent on `payment_id` and order-level capture

### WhatsApp (Meta)
- `GET /api/v1/whatsapp/webhook` — verification
- `POST /api/v1/whatsapp/webhook` — inbound messages

## Introduction

Configure Razorpay to send events to `https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/billing/webhook` and enable `payment.failed` for support visibility.

## Why it exists

Async payment and messaging systems must notify Qefro without polling.

## Concepts

- Signature verification
- Idempotent processing
- Acknowledge-even-on-unknown (where safe)

## Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant R as Razorpay/Meta
  participant WH as Qefro webhook
  participant DB as Postgres
  R->>WH: POST signed payload
  WH->>WH: Verify
  WH->>DB: Upsert state
  WH-->>R: 200
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```text
https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/billing/webhook
https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/whatsapp/webhook
```

## Best practices

- Keep webhook secrets only in server env
- Treat duplicate deliveries as normal (idempotency)

## Security notes

Reject unsigned payloads. Do not expose webhook URLs that skip verification.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Security

# Security Overview

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/security/overview/

> Tenant isolation, encrypted secrets, SSRF, identity forwarding, audit logs.

**Security Overview** — Tenant isolation, encrypted secrets, SSRF, identity forwarding, audit logs.

## Introduction

Qefro security controls in the running product:

- Multi-tenant isolation by `tenant_id`
- Workspace isolation for knowledge/tools/conversations
- Encrypted Business Tool secrets
- SSRF / DNS-pinned egress for tools and handoff webhooks
- End-user identity forwarding via `identify()`
- Org audit logs (`/api/v1/org/audit-logs`) and tool execution logs
- Auth abuse rate limits
- Razorpay webhook signature verification

SOC 2 and SSO/SAML are on the roadmap — contact Sales for timelines.

## Why it exists

Security documentation must reflect implemented controls, not aspirational marketing.

## Concepts

See [Security Overview](/docs/security/overview) for the control list.

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Tenant --> Workspace
  Workspace --> Secrets
  Workspace --> AuthZ
  AuthZ --> Logs
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/org/audit-logs
```

## Best practices

- Separate staging and production organizations when feasible
- Review OpenAPI imports for overly broad operations

## Security notes

Contact Sales for Enterprise compliance questionnaires and DPA process.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Tenant Isolation

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/security/tenant-isolation/

> How organizations and workspaces isolate data.

**Tenant Isolation** — How organizations and workspaces isolate data.

## Introduction

Every request resolves a tenant. Workspaces further isolate knowledge and tools. Cross-tenant access requires platform Super Admin capabilities — not tenant Admin roles.

## Why it exists

Security documentation must reflect implemented controls, not aspirational marketing.

## Concepts

See [Security Overview](/docs/security/overview) for the control list.

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Tenant --> Workspace
  Workspace --> Secrets
  Workspace --> AuthZ
  AuthZ --> Logs
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/org/audit-logs
```

## Best practices

- Separate staging and production organizations when feasible
- Review OpenAPI imports for overly broad operations

## Security notes

Contact Sales for Enterprise compliance questionnaires and DPA process.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Secrets

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/security/secrets/

> Encrypted credentials for Business Tools and channel configs.

**Secrets** — Encrypted credentials for Business Tools and channel configs.

## Introduction

Business Tool credentials are stored encrypted server-side. Widget tokens are publishable embed keys (rotate if leaked). Never place tool secrets in widget.js snippets.

## Why it exists

Security documentation must reflect implemented controls, not aspirational marketing.

## Concepts

See [Security Overview](/docs/security/overview) for the control list.

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Tenant --> Workspace
  Workspace --> Secrets
  Workspace --> AuthZ
  AuthZ --> Logs
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/org/audit-logs
```

## Best practices

- Separate staging and production organizations when feasible
- Review OpenAPI imports for overly broad operations

## Security notes

Contact Sales for Enterprise compliance questionnaires and DPA process.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Audit Logs

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/security/audit-logs/

> Organization audit trail and tool execution logs.

**Audit Logs** — Organization audit trail and tool execution logs.

## Introduction

Org audit: `GET /api/v1/org/audit-logs`. Tool executions: `GET /api/v1/tools/:id/logs`.

## Why it exists

Security documentation must reflect implemented controls, not aspirational marketing.

## Concepts

See [Security Overview](/docs/security/overview) for the control list.

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Tenant --> Workspace
  Workspace --> Secrets
  Workspace --> AuthZ
  AuthZ --> Logs
```

## Code examples

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/org/audit-logs
```

- Review OpenAPI imports for overly broad operations

Contact Sales for Enterprise compliance questionnaires and DPA process.

# Compliance

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/security/compliance/

> Roadmap items: SOC 2, SSO/SAML, Enterprise private deploy.

**Compliance** — Roadmap items: SOC 2, SSO/SAML, Enterprise private deploy.

## Introduction

Current public posture: strong isolation + encryption + auditability on the cloud product. Formal SOC 2 certification and SSO/SAML are roadmap items — email support@qefro.com / Sales for timelines. Enterprise private deployment is available as a custom engagement.

## Why it exists

Security documentation must reflect implemented controls, not aspirational marketing.

## Concepts

See [Security Overview](/docs/security/overview) for the control list.

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Tenant --> Workspace
  Workspace --> Secrets
  Workspace --> AuthZ
  AuthZ --> Logs
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER_JWT" \
  https://api.qefro.com/api/v1/org/audit-logs
```

## Best practices

- Separate staging and production organizations when feasible
- Review OpenAPI imports for overly broad operations

## Security notes

Contact Sales for Enterprise compliance questionnaires and DPA process.

## Related topics

# Compare

# Chatbase vs Qefro

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/compare/chatbase-vs-qefro/

> Factual comparison of Chatbase and Qefro for AI workspaces and customer/employee AI.

**Chatbase vs Qefro** compares chatbot builders focused on website Q&A with Qefro’s AI Workspace Platform (Customer AI, Employee AI, Business Actions).

## Introduction

This comparison is educational. Product capabilities change; verify details on each vendor’s site before purchasing. Qefro focuses on organization-wide AI Workspaces with shared knowledge, RBAC, and Business Actions — not on replacing every CRM or ticketing system of record.

## Capability matrix

## Why it exists

Buyers evaluating AI support tools need clear capability boundaries rather than slogans.

## Concepts

- **Qefro**: AI Workspaces, Customer AI, Employee AI, Business Tools/Actions, Internal Portal
- **Chatbase**: Chatbot builders focused on website Q&A
- Overlap often exists in conversational Q&A; divergence appears in employee portals, action execution, and workspace isolation models

## Architecture

Qefro centralizes Admin Console configuration and deploys multiple experiences from one knowledge/permission plane.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph Qefro
    WS[AI Workspaces]
    CA[Customer AI]
    EA[Employee AI]
    BA[Business Actions]
  end
  Other[Chatbase]
```

## Workflow

## Code examples

```json
{
  "evaluation_criteria": [
    "workspace_isolation",
    "employee_portal",
    "business_actions",
    "whatsapp",
    "rbac",
    "citations"
  ]
}
```

## Best practices

- Compare against your required channels and identity model
- Prefer read-only tool scopes during pilots
- Document gaps honestly for stakeholders

## Security notes

Do not migrate production secrets into a pilot until DPA/security review is complete for either vendor.

## FAQ

## Related topics

---

# Reference

# FAQ

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/faq/

> Frequently asked questions about the real Qefro product.

## Plans

## Related topics

---

# Glossary

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/glossary/

> Canonical Qefro terminology aligned with the product.

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| Organization / Tenant | Top-level account boundary for billing and isolation — see [Multi-tenant AI Architecture](/docs/concepts/multi-tenant-ai-architecture) |
| AI Workspace | Isolated knowledge + tools + conversations — see [What is an AI Workspace?](/docs/concepts/what-is-an-ai-workspace) |
| Customer AI | Website widget and/or WhatsApp assistants — see [Customer AI vs Employee AI](/docs/concepts/customer-ai-vs-employee-ai) |
| Employee AI | Internal Portal assistants for org members — see [Customer AI vs Employee AI](/docs/concepts/customer-ai-vs-employee-ai) |
| Knowledge Platform | Ingest + hybrid retrieval + citations — see [AI Knowledge Platform](/docs/concepts/ai-knowledge-platform) |
| Hybrid RAG | Lexical + vector retrieval for grounded answers — see [Hybrid RAG](/docs/concepts/hybrid-rag) |
| Business Tool | REST/OpenAPI connector with encrypted credentials |
| Business Action | Runtime invocation of a Business Tool — see [Business Actions](/docs/concepts/business-actions) |
| Identity Forwarding | identify() end-user JWT/session into tool calls |
| Widget token | Publishable embed key for cdn.qefro.com/widget.js |
| Internal Portal | your-company.qefro.com employee experience |
| Owner / Admin / Member | Organization RBAC roles |
| Team | Grants Members access to workspaces |

## Related topics

---

# Release Notes

Source: https://docs.qefro.com/docs/release-notes/

> Documentation and platform notes.

## 2026-07 — Docs aligned to production implementation

- Rewrote docs against real hosts: app.qefro.com, api.qefro.com, cdn.qefro.com
- Documented widget identify() transport headers and Business Tools REST routes
- Documented Razorpay webhooks including payment.failed and order-level idempotency
- Documented Owner/Admin/Member RBAC and org team APIs

## Related topics

---
